title: Cover failure cases, not just the happy path
description: The golden set deliberately includes hard, adversarial, and edge cases so the score reflects real-world robustness.
body: Use when assembling or auditing an eval set. Do not fill the set with easy, representative-only cases, and avoid a high average score that hides failures on the cases that matter. Actions: include known failure modes, adversarial inputs, rare-but-critical cases, and edge inputs; segment scores by case type rather than reporting one average. Validation signals include per-segment scores that expose weak categories rather than a single blended number. Risk boundary: an easy-only set produces a misleadingly high score that ships a fragile system; require failure-case coverage before trusting an aggregate.
